title: Therapeutic Drug Monitoring: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
ne levels in your blood. These may include:
Changes in your health. 
Any other medicines you take. 
Aging. 
For each of these changes, you may need to change your medicine dose. Otherwise, you might have too little medicine, which might not be effective, or too much (toxicity), which might not be safe.
TDM may also be used to make sure you are taking your medicine as prescribed.
There are many different medicines that are monitored; some of the most common ones include:
Types of Medicine 
Examples 
Antibiotics 
Amikacin, gentamycin, vancomycin 
Heart medicines 
Amiodarone, digoxin, lidocaine, procainamide 
Anti- seizure medicines 
Phenobarbital, phenytoin 
Medicines that treat autoimmune diseases 
Cyclosporine, tacrolimus 
Medicines that treat mental health conditions 
Lithium, valproic acid 
Medicines that treat fungal infections (antifungals) 
Itraconazole, voriconazole, posaconazole 
You may also need therapeutic drug monitoring if you are taking certain biologic medicines . This type of medicine is made from living cells instead of chemicals. These cells can be used to make proteins (like insulin), antibiotics, or altered versions of your cells. By working with specific proteins or cells in your immune system , biologic medicines can often treat conditions that traditional medicine cannot (such as cancer and autoimmune diseases ).
